title: The Palermo Exchange
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: A money launderer in Palermo loses a shipment of the Sicilian mafia's cash and has 72 hours to recover it or die.
long_description:
Luca Ferrara has spent seven years laundering money for Don Matteo Vitale, one of Palermo's most powerful mafia bosses. When a routine cash handoff goes wrong and two million euros vanish from the trunk of his car, Luca finds himself given an ultimatum: recover the money in seventy-two hours, or face execution.

As Luca races through Palermo's underworld following a trail of small-time crooks and middlemen, Inspector Chiara Rosi of the Postal Police picks up the same thread and begins closing in. What Luca discovers is that the theft was no accident — someone inside the organization set him up, and the betrayal runs deeper than he ever imagined.

The Palermo Exchange is a taut crime thriller about loyalty, betrayal, and the cost of surviving in a world where trust is the most dangerous currency. Set against the sun-baked streets of Palermo and the shadowy corridors of Sicilian finance, it follows one man's desperate race against time to save his own life.
main_characters: Luca Ferrara — A money launderer for the Vitale family who has seventy-two hours to recover two million euros in stolen mafia cash.
Don Matteo Vitale — The head of the Vitale mafia family, old-school Sicilian, who trusts no one and forgives nothing.
Inspector Chiara Rosi — A Postal Police investigator tracking Vitale's finances who sees Luca's desperation as her way in.
